title: Use chroot instead of pivot_root on the rootfs
body:
### Issue Description


As `pivot_root` is not supported on a rootfs, I am attempting to disable it f by passing the argument `--runtime-flag no-pivot` to the `podman` command.

However, the issue arises when using `--runtime-flag no-pivot`; the command is wrongly interpreted as:

```
DEBU[0000] Running ["/usr/bin/crun" "--no-pivot" "create" "--bundle" "/tmp/buildah718484688" "--pid-file" "/tmp/buildah718484688/pid" "--no-new-keyring" "buildah-buildah718484688"]
```

Instead of:

```
["/usr/bin/crun" "create" "--no-pivot" "--bundle" "/tmp/buildah718484688" "--pid-file" "/tmp/buildah718484688/pid" "--no-new-keyring" "buildah-buildah718484688"]
```

This results in the following error:

```
error running container: error from /usr/bin/crun creating container for [/bin/sh -c apt-get update]: /usr/bin/crun: unrecognized option '--no-pivot'
```

Is there any way to correctly pass the '--no-pivot' option to the runtime flags?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Use  `--runtime-flag no-pivot` with any podman build command.



### Describe the results you received

`error running container: error from /usr/bin/crun creating container for [/bin/sh -c apt-get update]: /usr/bin/crun: unrecognized option '--no-pivot'`


### Describe the results you expected

chroot should be used instead of pivot_root.

comments.body:
There is already a setting for this:

```toml
# Whether to use chroot instead of pivot_root in the runtime
#
#no_pivot_root = false

```

Note that it is more secure to copy the rootfs into a tmpfs at boot, than to try an workaround this limitation...

That is what we ended up doing for the boot2podman replacement, using `switch_root` after creation and copy.
as @afbjorklund pointed out, we already have a setting to disable pivot_root.  Please reopen the issue if that does not work for you

Does the placement of --runtime-flag XYZ make sense to add the option before or after the OCI Runtime flag?

$ociruntime --XYZ command ...
versus
$ociruntime command --XYZ ...
